ai_summary: Elon Musk promotes Grok, xAI's AI assistant product, with a direct link for users to try it. This represents a product launch/availability update for xAI's flagship conversational AI offering, demonstrating the company's push to bring its AI model to market as a competitor in the generative AI space.
embed_html:
Try Grok https://t.co/b4y96qxjFs

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) June 24, 2026

parent.text: STARMIND was just trademarked by @xai. Will this be the official name for the SpaceX AI satellite constellation?
parent.name: Robin
parent.embed_html:
STARMIND was just trademarked by @xai.

Will this be the official name for the SpaceX AI satellite constellation? pic.twitter.com/4x84zRGNZ0

— Robin (@xdNiBoR) June 23, 2026

ai_summary: xAI has trademarked 'STARMIND' as the official name for SpaceX's AI satellite constellation, representing a strategic initiative to combine SpaceX's satellite infrastructure with AI capabilities. This reveals xAI's expansion beyond software into satellite-based AI services, demonstrating Musk's strategy to integrate his AI company with SpaceX's Starlink network for potential global AI compute distribution.
embed_html:
Yes

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) June 23, 2026

text: @yunta_tsai Maybe first attempt at this for Neuralink later this year
summary: @yunta_tsai Maybe first attempt at this for Neuralink later this year

parent.text: Human-to-human interaction is often bandwidth-bound instead of compute-bound. Thus, the next evolutionary jump would be direct communication in latent space, skipping the long-latency encoder-decoder loop.
parent.name: Yun-Ta Tsai
parent.embed_html:
Human-to-human interaction is often bandwidth-bound instead of compute-bound.

Thus, the next evolutionary jump would be direct communication in latent space, skipping the long-latency encoder-decoder loop.

— Yun-Ta Tsai (@yunta_tsai) June 24, 2026

ai_summary: A technical discussion about human-computer interaction efficiency, proposing that direct communication in latent space (skipping traditional encoding/decoding) represents the next evolutionary step beyond bandwidth-limited human interaction. Elon responds that Neuralink is planning an early attempt at this technology later this year, connecting a theoretical AI/neuroscience concept to a concrete near-term product development milestone for his brain-computer interface company.
embed_html:
Maybe first attempt at this for Neuralink later this year

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) June 24, 2026
